label: Zipkin
description: Connect to a Zipkin server to query distributed traces and span data.

form.title: Connect Zipkin
form.subtitle: Point Anton at your Zipkin server to browse and query distributed traces.

methods.label: Zipkin Server URL
methods.description: Paste your Zipkin server's base URL. Works for self-hosted instances with no authentication and for hosted services where you also supply an API token.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Zipkin Server URL

This is the simplest path for both self-hosted and managed Zipkin deployments.

### Self-hosted Zipkin

1. Confirm your Zipkin server is running and reachable (default port is `9411`).
2. Copy the base URL, e.g. `http://localhost:9411` or `http://zipkin.internal:9411`.
3. Paste it into the **Server URL** field below.
4. Leave **API Token** blank — self-hosted Zipkin does not require one by default.
5. Click **Submit**. Anton will call `/api/v2/services` to verify connectivity.

### Hosted / managed Zipkin (e.g. Zipkin-as-a-service providers)

1. Log in to your hosted Zipkin dashboard.
2. Copy the base URL shown in the dashboard or settings page.
3. Locate your API token or access key (usually under **Settings → API Keys** or **Account → Access Tokens**).
4. Paste the URL into **Server URL** and the token into **API Token** below.
5. Click **Submit**.

### Notes

- The URL should NOT include a trailing path like `/api/v2` — Anton appends paths automatically.
- If your Zipkin is behind a reverse proxy with a custom base path, include that path in the URL (e.g. `https://observability.example.com/zipkin`).
- Ensure the server is reachable from the machine running Anton (firewall rules, VPN, etc.).

fields.name: server_url
fields.label: Server URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Zipkin server. Do not include a trailing slash or /api/v2 path.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Optional. Required only for hosted or secured Zipkin instances that enforce token-based authentication.

fields.name: api_version
fields.label: API Version
fields.description: Zipkin API version to use. v2 is recommended for all modern Zipkin deployments.

fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.label: Verify SSL certificate
fields.description: Disable only if your Zipkin server uses a self-signed certificate. Not recommended for production.

methods.label: Basic Authentication
methods.description: Connect to a Zipkin server secured with HTTP Basic Auth — provide the server URL along with a username and password.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Basic Authentication

Use this method if your Zipkin server (or the reverse proxy in front of it) is protected with HTTP Basic Auth.

### Steps

1. Obtain the base URL of your Zipkin server, e.g. `https://zipkin.example.com`.
2. Get the username and password configured on the server or proxy (check with your infrastructure or DevOps team).
3. Paste the URL into **Server URL**, and enter the credentials in **Username** and **Password**.
4. Click **Submit**. Anton will include an `Authorization: Basic ...` header on every request.

### Notes

- Basic Auth credentials are stored locally in Anton's encrypted vault.
- If your proxy uses a different scheme (digest, mutual TLS, etc.), use the **Server URL** method with a token or contact your admin.
- Disable **Verify SSL** only if you're on a private network with a self-signed cert — otherwise leave it on.

fields.name: server_url

fields.description: Base URL of your Zipkin server. Do not include a trailing slash or /api/v2 path.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: HTTP Basic Auth username configured on your Zipkin server or reverse proxy.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: HTTP Basic Auth password. Stored encrypted in Anton's local vault.
